what_it_does: Defines a single RELATION_TYPE dict for the 'references' board edge: a directed cross-reference linking any address to any address (this artifact references / is-compared-against / builds-on that one), with inverse 'referenced_by'.
observations: desc describes any-address-to-any-address links (image://<id> to image://<src>, image to text, attachment to attachment).
desc names the comparison workflow: link a generated image to its source image, then traverse to pair them.
standouts: The broadest/most generic edge among the siblings (any address to any address).
Carries the 'generated output vs source' comparison-pairing use case.
notes: The general-purpose directed cross-reference edge; siblings like composes_with/refutes exist precisely so this one isn't overloaded.
questions: What distinguishes 'references' from 'is-compared-against' and 'builds-on' if all three map to this one edge?
purpose_vs_actual: Match: desc describes a generic directional cross-reference and the dict declares it.
intention_signals: Trying to provide the catch-all directional link between any two addressed artifacts, especially to pair a generated output with its source for comparison.
novelty: Its breadth (any address to any address) and the explicit generated-vs-source comparison pairing use case.
contribution: Contributes the general directional cross-reference edge with its referenced_by inverse and the output-to-source comparison-pairing pattern.
summary_for_embedding: A board relation-type definition for 'references', a directed any-address-to-any-address cross-reference edge (inverse referenced_by) used to pair artifacts such as a generated image and its source.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a general directional link between any two addressed artifacts so outputs can be paired with their sources for comparison.
